about.h2Line2: Run today by Erick, the second generation.
about.body1: Sunset Services has been mowing lawns, building patios, and clearing snow in DuPage County for 25+ years. Same family, same crew of long-tenure workers, same 6:30am start.
about.body2: Erick took the company over from Nick in 2018, then launched the Unilock-authorized hardscape division in 2021. Everything else stayed.
about.alt: Erick Valle, owner of Sunset Services, on a job site in Aurora.

cards.body: No outside ownership group, no quarterly quota. Decisions stay with the people doing the work.

cards.label: Fair & transparent pricing

cards.label: HOA & permit experience

cards.label: Professional design guidance

cards.label: Long-term customer relationships
cards.body: Many year-one customers from 2000 are still on the route today. Reputation is built one homeowner at a time.

cards.label: Focused on value — not volume
cards.body: We schedule by quality, not by quota. Fewer rushed jobs; more attention to each project.

cta.body: Tell us about the property and we'll come measure, sketch, and price it. Free, no obligation, usually within a week.

meta.title: Landscape Services in DuPage County — Sunset Services
meta.description: Lawn care, landscape design, sprinkler systems, tree services, and seasonal cleanups for homes and businesses across DuPage County. 25+ years.

hero.h1: Yards that look intentional in every season.
hero.subhead: Lawn care, design, sprinklers, trees, and seasonal cleanups — eight services, one crew. Residential and commercial properties across DuPage County.
hero.alt: A lush front yard with crisp edging, mature plantings, and a paver walkway at golden hour.

qualifier.body: If you want one team handling the lawn, the beds, the irrigation, and the seasonal work — without juggling three vendors — this page is for you. Same crew every visit, bilingual leads, free 48-hour estimate.

servicesGrid.sub: Pick a service to see what's included, our process, and recent projects.

one.headline: 25+ years on DuPage
one.description: Family-run since 2000. We've seen the seasons.

two.headline: Same crew every visit
two.description: Your lead won't change. Quality stays consistent.

three.headline: Bilingual team · EN · ES
three.description: Crew leads speak both. Quotes available in either.

four.headline: One contact across services
four.description: Lawn, design, snow — single phone number, single person.

one.q: Do you offer one-time visits or only contracts?
one.a: Both. Most lawn-care customers prefer a seasonal contract for consistency, but we'll do one-time cleanups, design consults, and project work without a recurring commitment.

two.a: Lawn-care and seasonal cleanup usually start within two weeks. Design and bed work depends on scope — most book within four to six weeks of estimate sign-off.

three.a: Yes — site visit, measurements, and itemized estimate are free. Quotes land in your inbox within 48 hours of the visit.

four.a: Yes — about a third of our landscape book is commercial. We do property maintenance, turf management, and seasonal enhancements on quarterly cadence.

five.a: Yes — $2M general liability plus full workers' comp. Insurance certificates available on request before the first visit.

cta.body: Free estimate within 48 hours. No high-pressure sales — just a clear quote.

meta.title: Hardscape & Unilock Pavers in DuPage County — Sunset Services
meta.description: Patios, walls, fire features, outdoor kitchens, pergolas, and driveways by a Unilock Authorized Contractor. 5-year installation warranty. 25+ years.

hero.h1: Built to last twenty-five years and counting.
hero.subhead: Patios, walls, fire features, outdoor kitchens, pergolas, and driveways — installed by a Unilock Authorized Contractor with two decades of paver experience.
hero.alt: A Unilock paver patio with seating wall and fire feature at sunset.

qualifier.body: If you want a patio, walkway, retaining wall, or outdoor kitchen built by a Unilock Authorized Contractor — engineering reviewed, base prep in-house, and a 5-year installation warranty — this page is for you.

servicesGrid.sub: Pick a service to see what's included, our process, and recent installs.

one.headline: Unilock Authorized Contractor
one.description: Awarded — not bought. Earned by proven install quality.

two.headline: 5-year installation warranty
two.description: Workmanship covered five years, no questions asked.

three.headline: In-house design
three.description: Designer, engineer, and crew lead under one roof.

four.headline: Seasoned paver crew
four.description: Marcin's team brings deep Unilock paver experience.

unilock.body: Unilock Authorization is awarded — not bought — to contractors who can prove consistent installation quality. We've held our authorization through years of proven work.

one.q: How long does a typical patio take?
one.a: A 400–600 sq ft residential paver patio runs 5–8 days from break-ground to walkthrough. Larger scopes or weather-impacted weeks can stretch to two weeks.

two.a: 5-year workmanship warranty on the install. Unilock's manufacturer warranty on the pavers themselves runs separately and depends on the paver line.

three.q: Do you do design before construction?
three.a: Yes — most hardscape projects start with a concept conversation, scaled drawings, and a materials walkthrough. Design and build are separate engagements; you keep the plans either way.

four.q: What materials do you typically use?
four.a: Unilock primarily — as a Unilock Authorized Contractor. We can also work with Belgard or other premium lines on request, though our crew is most efficient on Unilock systems.

five.q: How does your project schedule work?
five.a: Spring builds typically book by late February; summer/fall slots open through April. We confirm a start window inside the quote and lock the date once the deposit clears.

cta.body: Free site walk and concept sketch within a week. Permitting and engineering folded into the quote.

meta.title: Waterproofing & Foundation Services in DuPage County — Sunset Services
meta.description: Basement waterproofing, foundation repair, sump pumps, yard drainage, gutters, window wells, crawl spaces, concrete raising, humidity control, and radon mitigation.

hero.subhead: Ten services for water management around the house — basements, foundations, sump pumps, yard drainage, gutters, window wells, and more. Diagnose first, then fix.
hero.alt: An exterior view of a DuPage home foundation with new drainage tile installed at the perimeter.

qualifier.body: If your basement leaks every spring, your foundation has hairline cracks, your yard puddles after rain, or your crawl space smells musty — this page is for you. We diagnose the source before we sell you a solution.

servicesGrid.sub: Pick a service to see what's included, our process, and recent jobs.

one.headline: Diagnose first
one.description: We find the actual source of water before we recommend a fix.

two.headline: 5–25-year warranties
two.description: Per service. We tell you the range before we quote.

three.headline: Old-house experienced
three.description: Brick foundations, century homes, additions on additions.

four.headline: Same crew, same accountability
four.description: No subcontractor handoffs on the work that matters most.

one.q: How do I know if my basement actually has a leak?
one.a: A real leak shows up after a heavy rain — water at the floor edges or staining on the walls. Condensation alone (musty smell, occasional dampness in summer) is a humidity issue and the fix is different. We can tell which it is during the site visit.

two.q: Will fixing my basement require digging up my yard?
two.a: Sometimes. Interior drain tile installs from inside the basement with no excavation. Exterior membranes do require digging the foundation perimeter — usually a 3-5 day job, and we restore the landscape after.

three.q: How long do your fixes last?
three.a: Interior drain tile and sump systems carry 25-year warranties when installed by us. Exterior membranes 10-15 years. Foundation crack repair 5-10 years depending on the crack.

four.q: Can you fix a yard that floods every spring?
four.a: Yes — yard drainage is one of our ten waterproofing services. We map low spots, install French drains or surface drains, and route the water to a daylight outlet. Most jobs done in 2-3 days.

five.a: Yes — site visit, diagnosis, and itemized estimate are free. We don't quote without walking the property; phone-only quotes for waterproofing tend to be wrong.

cta.body: Free site walk and itemized estimate within 48 hours. We diagnose before we quote.

meta.title: Snow Removal in DuPage County — Sunset Services
meta.description: Driveway plowing, sidewalk shoveling, de-icing, and commercial snow plowing for DuPage homes, HOAs, and offices. 24/7 dispatch, 2-hour response after trigger.

hero.h1: Plowed before you're out of bed.
hero.subhead: Driveways, sidewalks, de-icing, commercial lots — four services covering every snow scenario in DuPage County. 24/7 dispatch, 2-hour response after the trigger.
hero.alt: A freshly plowed driveway with snow piled cleanly off to one side at dawn after an overnight storm.

qualifier.body: If you want a snow vendor that actually shows up — single-family driveway, HOA roadways, office park, multi-property contract — this page is for you. 2-inch trigger, automatic dispatch, no per-storm phone calls.

servicesGrid.sub: Pick a service to see what's included, our process, and how the contract works.

one.headline: 2-hour response
one.description: You're served before most people are out of bed.

two.headline: 24/7 dispatch
two.description: Storm at 2am? We're already on the way.

three.headline: Insured fleet
three.description: Property-damage coverage on every plow on the road.

four.headline: Apron-return pass
four.description: Second pass after the village plow comes through.

one.a: Most contracts sign before Halloween for the upcoming winter. We mark drives, walks, and sprinkler heads in November so the crew knows the property before the first storm.

two.a: 2 inches by default. Below 2 inches we don't service residential. Commercial contracts can specify a lower trigger (1 inch or zero-trigger ice management) for an additional fee.

three.a: 2 hours from trigger fire on residential. 2 hours by contracted SLA on commercial. We dispatch in waves — residential routes run after the commercial accounts.

four.a: Yes — de-icing is a standalone service. Pet-safe melt on residential, calibrated salt or eco-melt on commercial per the contract spec.

five.a: Seasonal contracts have no mid-season cancellation; the price assumes a full season's work. Per-event service is pay-as-you-go and you can stop any time.

cta.body: Free estimate within 48 hours. Most contracts sign in September. Don't wait for the first storm.

meta.title: Trenchless Drilling & Directional Boring in DuPage County — Sunset Services
meta.description: Conduit installation, trenching, sewer line replacement, missile boring, handhole and pull-box installation, and HDPE pipe fusing across DuPage County. We locate before we dig. Free 48-hour estimates.

hero.h1: Lines in the ground. Yard intact.
hero.subhead: Conduit, sewer laterals, and utility crossings — installed by directional boring and open-cut trenching. Six services for getting lines in the ground across DuPage County. We locate before we dig, and restore what we touch.
hero.alt: Underground utility installation on a DuPage County property at golden hour.

qualifier.body: If you need power or data run to a detached structure, a failed sewer lateral replaced, or a utility line crossed under the driveway without tearing it up — this page is for you. We locate before we dig and put the surface back the way we found it.

servicesGrid.sub: Pick a service to see what's included, our process, and recent jobs.

one.headline: Locate before we dig
one.description: 811 every job — marked, verified, and hand-dug near the lines.

two.headline: Trenchless or open-cut
two.description: We pick the method that protects your driveway and landscaping.

three.headline: Same crew, one contact
three.description: One accountable lead from estimate to final walkthrough.

four.headline: Licensed, insured, bilingual
four.description: A DuPage family business since 2000. Crews speak EN · ES.

one.q: What's the difference between trenchless and open-cut?
one.a: Trenchless methods — directional boring, missile boring, pipe bursting — install lines underground from small pits, so we don't trench the whole run. Open-cut digs the full length. We pick whichever protects your property and fits the job, and tell you which during the estimate.

two.q: Will you tear up my driveway or lawn?
two.a: Usually not. A bore crosses under driveways, walks, and landscaping from two small pits. Where open-cut is the right call, we restore the surface — soil, gravel, or sod — once the work is in.

three.a: Every time. We file the 811 locate, wait for utilities to be marked, and hand-dig around marked lines. Locate-before-dig isn't optional on our jobs.

four.q: Can you replace a sewer line without digging up the whole yard?
four.a: Often, yes. We camera the lateral first, then use trenchless pipe bursting where the run allows — pulling new pipe along the old path. Where it doesn't, we dig, replace, and restore after.

five.a: Yes — site visit, utility-locate planning, and an itemized estimate are free, back to you within 48 hours. We don't quote underground work without seeing the site.

cta.body: Free site visit and itemized estimate within 48 hours. We locate before we dig — and quote before we start.

meta.title: Accessibility statement · Sunset Services
meta.description: Sunset Services' commitment to making our website accessible to everyone, with WCAG 2.2 AA as our standard.

commitment.p: Sunset Services is committed to making our website usable by everyone, including visitors who navigate with assistive technology. We treat the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 Level AA as our floor, not our ceiling — accessibility is a craft we tune every release.

doneSoFar.intro: We design and build with accessibility from the first wireframe:
doneSoFar.item1: Semantic HTML and ARIA labels for landmark navigation
doneSoFar.item2: Full keyboard navigation across every page; visible focus indicators on every interactive element
doneSoFar.item3: Screen reader testing on representative routes
doneSoFar.item4: Color contrast ratios verified against the WCAG 2.2 AA standard in our Phase B.06 audit (zero AA violations, Lighthouse a11y = 100)
doneSoFar.item5: Animated content respects your prefers-reduced-motion setting
doneSoFar.item6: Form labels, error messages, and instructions tested with assistive technology

feedback.p: If anything on this site isn't working for you — a keyboard trap, a missing label, a contrast issue, a confusing flow — please tell us. Email <emailLink>info@sunsetservices.us</emailLink> or call <phoneLink>(630) 946-9321</phoneLink> and we'll respond within two business days.

lastReviewed.p: This statement was last reviewed in April 2026 and is updated whenever a notable change ships.

meta.title: About Sunset Services — Family-run since 2000 · DuPage
meta.description: Twenty-five years on DuPage County properties. Meet Erick, Nick, and Marcin — the Aurora family running Sunset Services since 2000.

lead.line1: Twenty-five years on DuPage County properties.
lead.line2: Same crews. Same 6:30 a.m. start. No subcontracted surprises.

hero.alt: Erick on a DuPage County job site at golden hour, hardscape installation in progress.

story.p1: Nick Valle started Sunset Services in 2000 with one truck and a maintenance route across Aurora and Naperville. The first contract is still active, twenty-five years later. We've stayed locally owned and family operated since day one — based in Aurora, IL.
story.p2: In 2018, Erick — Nick's son — took over operations. Same crew, same 6:30 a.m. start, same standard for what a finished property looks like. We answer to our customers, not to outside ownership groups or private-equity quotas. No subcontracted surprises; the people who measure your project are the people who do it.
story.p3: Marcin joined to lead the hardscape division — Unilock-Authorized installs, paver patios, retaining walls, fire features. The standard is exceptional workmanship, fair pricing, and a finished property you can hand off to the family with confidence. The trucks share a yard; your job stays one phone call long.
story.p4: Our goal isn't to sell projects. It's to help homeowners make smart investments — outdoor spaces families can enjoy for years to come. Our reputation is built one homeowner, one neighborhood, one project at a time.
story.alt: Erick and Nick working on-site together at a DuPage County property, late afternoon light.

team.h2: The people you'd be working with.

erick.bio: Took over operations in 2018. Runs every estimate himself — you meet him before you sign anything.

nick.bio: Started Sunset Services in 2000. Still on-site most weeks. The senior eye on the complicated jobs.

marcin.bio: Leads the Unilock-Authorized hardscape division. Patios, walls, fire features — specs to install.

faq.title: Quick questions
faq.q1: How soon will I hear back?
faq.a1: Within one business day. If you submitted on a Friday afternoon, expect a call Monday morning. Voicemail and a follow-up email always go out the same business day.
faq.a2: A rough idea of your timeline and budget, plus any photos or sketches you have. Don't worry if you only have half of it — we'll help fill in the rest on the call.
faq.a3: Of course — call (630) 946-9321 or reply to the confirmation email and we'll find another time.

canned.prompt1Reply: A paver patio in our area runs $18–32 per sq ft installed, depending on materials and base prep. A typical 200 sq ft patio lands around $4,500–$6,500. Want a free walk-through with Erick to scope yours?

meta.title: Common questions — Sunset Services
meta.description: Straight answers to the 28 questions Chicago-area homeowners ask most about landscape, hardscape, waterproofing, and snow removal.

hero.dek: 28 questions Chicago-area homeowners ask us most often — planning, pricing, materials, drainage, outdoor living, maintenance, and credentials. Don't see yours? Call (630) 946-9321.

questions.q: How does the design and estimate process work?
questions.a: We begin with a site consultation to review your property, discuss goals, evaluate drainage and elevation concerns, and explore material options. We then prepare a detailed proposal and design recommendations tailored to your space and budget.

questions.q: Do I need a plat of survey?
questions.a: In many cases, yes — especially for patios, retaining walls, pavilions, pergolas, driveways, and HOA submissions. A plat helps us verify layout, property lines, setbacks, and permit requirements.

questions.q: Will Sunset Services help with HOA approvals?
questions.a: Yes. We regularly work with HOA communities throughout the western suburbs and can assist with architectural review submissions, material sheets, plats, and supporting documentation.

questions.q: Do you handle permits?
questions.a: Yes. When required, we assist with permit applications and coordinate with local municipalities to help ensure the project is compliant.

questions.q: How far out are you scheduling projects?
questions.a: Scheduling varies throughout the season. Spring and summer projects often book several weeks or months in advance, so early planning is recommended.

questions.q: Why are hardscape projects expensive?
questions.a: Quality hardscape installations require extensive excavation, proper base preparation, drainage planning, premium materials, skilled labor, equipment, disposal costs, and long-term structural considerations. Proper installation protects against settling, movement, and premature failure.

questions.q: Why is Sunset Services not the cheapest contractor?
questions.a: We focus on long-term quality, communication, professionalism, and proper installation methods — not shortcuts. Our goal is to build projects that last and elevate your property value.

questions.q: Do you offer financing?
questions.a: Yes. Financing options may be available for qualified customers through third-party financing partners.

questions.q: What deposit is required?
questions.a: Most projects require a deposit to secure scheduling and material procurement. Final payment terms are outlined within each proposal.

questions.q: What paver brands do you use?
questions.a: We primarily install premium products from Unilock and other high-quality manufacturers known for durability, aesthetics, and long-term performance.

questions.q: What is the difference between concrete and pavers?
questions.a: Pavers offer superior flexibility, easier repairability, stronger visual appeal, and better long-term performance in freeze/thaw climates compared to standard poured concrete.

questions.q: What is a permeable paver system?
questions.a: Permeable systems allow water to infiltrate through the joints into a specialized stone base below, helping with drainage, runoff reduction, and winter performance.

questions.q: Do pavers settle or move over time?
questions.a: Any outdoor surface can shift if improperly installed. Proper excavation, base preparation, drainage management, and edge restraint systems are critical to long-term performance.

questions.q: Do you use polymeric sand?
questions.a: Yes. Polymeric sand helps stabilize joints, reduce weed growth, and minimize insect intrusion while improving overall appearance.

questions.q: Why is drainage so important for retaining walls?
questions.a: Improper drainage creates hydrostatic pressure behind walls, which can eventually lead to movement or failure. We incorporate proper drainage practices to help protect the structure.

questions.q: Can you solve water or drainage issues?
questions.a: Yes. We regularly design drainage solutions including downspout extensions, catch basins, dry creek beds, grading improvements, and French drain systems.

questions.q: Do retaining walls require permits?
questions.a: Depending on wall height, location, and municipality, permits may be required. We help guide customers through that process.

questions.q: Do you build outdoor kitchens and entertainment spaces?
questions.a: Yes. We design and install outdoor kitchens, fire features, seating walls, lighting systems, pergolas, entertainment areas, and custom outdoor living environments.

questions.q: Can lighting be added to existing patios or walls?
questions.a: In many cases, yes. We install landscape and hardscape lighting including under-coping lights, stair lighting, accent lighting, and roofline systems.

questions.q: Do you install pergolas and pavilions?
questions.a: Yes. We install both attached and freestanding structures depending on the project layout and municipal requirements.

questions.q: Do you provide a warranty?
questions.a: Yes. Sunset Services provides a workmanship warranty on qualifying installations. Warranty details are outlined within each proposal.

questions.q: Should I seal my pavers?
questions.a: Not always. Some surfaces benefit from sealing while others may not require it. We recommend sealing based on the specific material, age, and condition of the installation.

questions.q: What maintenance is required after installation?
questions.a: We recommend occasional cleaning, joint sand maintenance when necessary, and avoiding harsh deicing salts during the first winter on new concrete installations.

questions.q: What happens if another contractor repairs or modifies the project?
questions.a: Work performed by others may void workmanship warranty coverage on affected areas.

questions.q: Is Sunset Services insured?
questions.a: Yes. We are fully insured for residential and commercial work.

questions.q: Are you a certified contractor?
questions.a: Yes. Sunset Services is proud to be a Unilock Authorized Contractor and holds additional hardscape-related certifications.

questions.q: What areas do you service?
questions.a: We primarily serve the western suburbs of the Chicago area including Aurora, Naperville, St. Charles, Geneva, Batavia, Downers Grove, Elmhurst, Barrington, and surrounding communities.

questions.q: Can I see examples of your work?
questions.a: Yes. Visit our gallery and our social pages to see completed projects, renderings, and ongoing installations.

cta.sub: Get a free estimate or give us a call — we'll talk through your project, no pressure.
